On July , 2025 at 6:47 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a bus on near . 
The Waymo AV was parked in-lane for a passenger pick-up facing south on with its hazard lights activated. A bus traveling southbound on began to maneuver around the stationary Waymo AV by crossing the yellow center line, entering the opposing lane of travel. As the bus proceeded to the left of the stationary Waymo AV, the right side of the bus made contact with the rear left side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On October , 2023 at 3:11 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in San Francisco, CA was in a collision involving a passenger car on near . 
The Waymo AV was traveling slowly (less than 5 miles per hour) west on near in heavy pedestrian traffic in the street when it stopped to yield to a crossing pedestrian. The front of the passenger car traveling behind the Waymo AV made contact with the rear of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01.

text:
On February , 2026 at 7:09 AM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Scottsdale, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger car on near .

The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on in the rightmost lane. A passenger car traveling northbound in the second to the left lane, in front of and to the left of the Waymo AV, began to slow and change lanes to the right when a second passenger car made contact with the rear right side of the first passenger car. The second passenger car continued forward and made contact with an SUV stopped in the queue of traffic in the second from the left lane. As a result of the initial contact with the second passenger car, the first passenger car was pushed east across two lanes of travel, and entered the Waymo AV's lane of travel. The Waymo AV slowed and maneuvered to the right to avoid the oncoming passenger car and the front left side of the passenger car made contact with the left side of the Waymo AV. The Waymo AV rotated to the left and came to rest facing southwest in the second and third from the left lanes. During two subsequent maneuvers in which the Waymo AV pulled forward and to the right in an attempt to navigate off the freeway, the front left corner of the Waymo AV made contact with an "Oversized Load" sign attached to a heavy truck stopped in the second from the left lane. At the time of all impacts, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. All involved vehicles sustained damage. The driver of the first passenger car was transported to a hospital by an ambulance with unknown injuries, and the passenger of the Waymo AV alleged minor injuries. 

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because an individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital for medical treatment. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On December , 2025 at 1:17 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision involving a van on near .
The Waymo AV was stopped facing west on for a red light at the intersection with with its right turn signal activated. After remaining stopped for approximately six minutes a short distance in advance of the stop line at the red light, the Waymo AV passenger opened the rear left side door and exited the vehicle, and a van that was initially stopped directly behind the Waymo AV maneuvered to the left before slowing to a stop behind the Waymo AV. The Waymo AV passenger briefly interacted with the van operator through the van window, and then re-entered the Waymo AV. The van then proceeded to pass the Waymo AV on the left and the right side of the van made contact with the rear left side of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage. The passenger in the Waymo AV was not belted at the time of the collision.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On March , 2023 at 7:00 PM PST a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Tempe, Arizona was in a collision involving a passenger vehicle on near .
The Waymo AV was traveling southbound on behind traffic that was backed up from the intersection with . As the Waymo AV was accelerating from a stop, a passenger vehicle approached the Waymo AV from behind and made contact with the rear of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and. a test driver was present (in the driver's seating position). The Waymo AV test driver reported having a minor injury and the passenger vehicle left the scene.
Transdev Alternative Services is reporting this crash under Request no. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Transdev may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available. This report is a duplicate of 30270-5324-1.

On May , 2025 at 8:00 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision involving a cyclist on at . 
The Waymo AV came to a stop facing westbound on at a stop sign at the intersection at in a location where vegetation to the left of the AV created occlusion of the approaching near-side sidewalk. After coming to a stop at the stop line, the Waymo AV began moving forward into the intersection at when a cyclist that had been traveling northwest on the near-side sidewalk along entered the crosswalk and the front tire of the bike made contact with the front driver side of the Waymo AV. The cyclist fell to the ground. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The cyclist later informed Waymo of a minor injury.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vulnerable road user influenced the driving task of a vehicle involved. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text: The May vehicle was involved in a rear-end collision shortly after completing a right-hand turn. As the vehicle proceeded through its turn, it encountered exhaust fumes from vehicles waiting at a stoplight in the adjacent lane. Due to environmental conditions and sensor limitations, the vehicle's lidar system interpreted the exhaust plume as a solid object in its path. In response, the autonomous vehicle first tried to adjust its position. When this maneuver proved insufficient, the system initiated a hard stop response. The vehicle behind, which was following at approximately 7-8 meters, was unable to stop in time and collided with the rear of the autonomous vehicle. The collision occurred at relatively low speeds (the autonomous vehicle had decelerated from approximately 7.6 m/s to 0 m/s), though exact impact speeds of the following vehicle were not recorded. The primary factor that led to this incident is that Lidar does not pierce through exhaust so the exhaust cloud appears to be a large, solid object. Some corrective actions that will be implemented: consider weather implication in future platform sensor selection and continue working on the software to improve a neural network to classify lidar points, which will use temporal information, radar, and camera features to make a decision on whether a lidar point is truly an object.

On January , 2023 at 2:19 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in Phoenix, Arizona was in a collision involving an SUV on at .
The Waymo AV was traveling northbound on and approaching the intersection with with a green traffic light. As the Waymo AV approached the intersection, it detected a SUV towing a trailer traveling westbound on at a high rate of speed. The Waymo AV braked as the SUV approached and entered the intersection against a red traffic light. The Waymo AV made contact with the driver side of the SUV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage and the SUV left the scene.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On February , 2025 at 4:31 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a van on . 
The Waymo AV was traveling north in the second from the right lane on and came to a stop in a queue of traffic for a red light at the intersection at . As the light at turned green, the Waymo AV began to proceed and then approached a stopped school bus in the rightmost lane. The school bus did not have active flashing lights, but the Waymo AV came to a stop due to the detection of possible flashing lights on the school bus. A van approached the Waymo AV from behind, and the front of the van made contact with the rear of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

text: Manual control was initiated by a May autonomous shuttle operator for a right turn onto in Atlanta, GA. Autonomous mode was then engaged. The autonomous system executed a stop due to a narrow passage obstructed by parked vehicles on both sides of the roadway and an approaching vehicle. Following the departure of the first vehicle and the next oncoming vehicle 80m away, the Autonomous Vehicle Operator (AVO) disengages and proceeds in manual. The AVO overcorrects to the right slightly and a minor contact with the parked car's mirror occurs. Our team looked at the incident and determined that this event was caused by human error - no autonomy software defects led to this incident.

On January , 2026 at 1:18 PM MT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Denver, Colorado was in a collision involving a pickup truck on near .

The Waymo AV was traveling eastbound through a construction zone on in autonomous mode when it began to maneuver to the right, towards the south curb. As the Waymo AV was maneuvering, the test driver transitioned the Waymo AV to manual mode. The test driver began maneuvering the Waymo AV closer to the curb, briefly accelerated forward, and then began braking. The front of the Waymo AV then made contact with the rear of a pickup truck that was parked facing east at the curb in front of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was not engaged and a test driver was operating the Waymo AV in manual mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On August , 2024 at 7:15 AM EDT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Atlanta, Georgia was in a collision involving a fallen utility line on near .
The Waymo AV was traveling eastbound on when the front of the Waymo AV made contact with a fallen utility line hanging from a utility pole on the right edge of the roadway into the travel lane and laying on the ground across the left half of the roadway. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode, and a test driver was present in the driver's seating position. The Waymo AV sustained damage from contact with the non-live line.
Transdev Alternative Services is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Transdev may supplement or correct it resporting with additional information as it may become available. This is a duplicate of report ID 30270-8443-1.

text: A Zoox retrofitted test vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was stopped, yielding to cross-traffic, on westbound at in Miami while waiting for an opportunity to proceed through the intersection and continue west on . The Zoox vehicle inched forward and then stopped again for cross-traffic. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomous mode and proceeded to begin to cross . While crossing, a motorcyclist traveling northbound on at a high rate of speed collided with the driver's side front fender and door of the Zoox vehicle. The motorcycle and its operator fell to the ground. After the collision, the Zoox vehicle operator cleared the intersection and pulled over. Both the Zoox vehicle and the motorcycle sustained damage. Police and EMT personnel arrived at the scene, but the motorcycle operator was not transported from the scene.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) operating in autonomous mode was traveling southeast on , slowing gradually in preparation for a right turn onto in San Francisco, with turn signal illuminated. At that time, an e-scooterist was traveling southeast at a high speed in the adjacent bike lane, rapidly approaching on the right of the Zoox vehicle from behind in an apparent attempt to overtake the Zoox vehicle. The e-scooterist veered suddenly to overtake on the left of the Zoox vehicle, and the Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy and completed braking to a stop. The e-scooterist crossed just behind the left side of the Zoox vehicle's rear bumper and made a glancing contact with the Zoox vehicle. The e-scooterist crossed over the centerline, entered the oncoming lane of traffic, and then apparently made contact with the driver's side rear of an SUV that was traveling northwest along . The e-scooterist remained upright, and the SUV braked to a stop after contact. The Zoox vehicle operator completed the turn onto and pulled over. The e-scooterist picked up their e-scooter, and then walked it to the sidewalk. After a brief conversation with the Zoox vehicle operator the e-scooterist rode away from the scene. The Zoox vehicle did not sustain any damage, but the SUV may have sustained damage. There were no reported injuries and the police were not notified.
The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

On July , 2025 at 7:35 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in Los Angeles, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on at . 
The Waymo AV was traveling northeastbound in the third from the right lane on and attempting to merge into the left turn lane when it stopped to yield to a queue of traffic that filled the left turn lane at the intersection at . A passenger car traveling northeastbound on in the third from the right lane attempted to change lanes to the right as it approached the Waymo AV, and the front driver side of the passenger car made contact with the rear passenger side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On August , 2025 at 4:27 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a motorcycle on near . 
The Waymo AV was parked facing south in the leftmost lane on for a passenger drop-off with hazard lights activated. While the Waymo AV remained parked, a motorcycle that was traveling southbound in the center lane on approached a queue of stopped traffic. The motorcycle began to change lanes to the leftmost lane as it approached the stopped traffic and the motorcycle made contact with the rear right of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both the motorcycle and the Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On March , 2025 at 7:04 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a passenger car in a parking lot (for BoConcept) on near . 
The Waymo AV was stopped in a parking lot on for a requested passenger pickup with a moped parked at the curb directly in front and parked vehicles in parking stalls to the adjacent right. After the Waymo AV completed the passenger pickup, it reversed briefly to create space between itself and the moped before slowing to a stop. While the Waymo AV remained stopped, a passenger car to its right began to reverse out of a parking stall. The rear of the passenger car then made contact with the front passenger side of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On September , 2025 at 5:41 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on at . 
The Waymo AV was stopped facing north on for a stop sign at the intersection at . While the Waymo AV remained stopped to yield to pedestrians crossing the street, a passenger car with hazard lights activated approached the Waymo AV from behind and the front of the passenger car made contact with the rear of the stationary Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. Both vehicles sustained damage.

Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

On January , 2024 at 12:35 PM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle (Waymo AV) operating in Gilbert, Arizona was in an event involving parking lot spikes at a parking lot entrance on near .
The Waymo AV had approached an apartment complex parking lot entrance on near , and encountered a closed gate, preventing access to the parking lot. Next to the closed entrance gate lane was an open exit lane, with parking lot spikes preventing wrong-way access. As the Waymo AV attempted a multi-point turn to exit the parking lot driveway and proceed on , the front passenger side tire drove over the parking lot spikes and deflated. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AVs Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this under Request No. 1 of Standing General Order 2021-01 because a vehicle involved was towed away. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.

The Waymo AV was parked at the curb on northwestbound when a passenger car in front of the Waymo AV began to reverse. The rear of the passenger car made contact with the front of the Waymo AV and the passenger car left the scene. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode. The Waymo AV sustained damage.
Waymo is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01. Waymo may supplement or correct its reporting with additional information as it may become available.
